steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The passage states that modern planes have sleeker, more streamlined bodies and then immediately makes a claim about aerodynamics. Ask: is the second sentence a contrast, an addition, a conclusion, or something else? Because streamlined bodies directly cause better aerodynamics, the second sentence is a result of the first — the blank needs a cause-and-effect transition.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong transitions and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — "However,": signals contrast or a surprising counterpoint. The aerodynamic improvement is not surprising given the streamlined design — it is the expected result. ✗
  • Choice B — "Nevertheless,": signals that something is true despite an obstacle. No obstacle is present here; streamlined bodies straightforwardly produce better aerodynamics. ✗
  • Choice C — "Lastly,": signals the final item in an enumerated list. The passage is not listing characteristics — it is stating a causal outcome. ✗
  • Choice D — "For this reason,": signals that what follows is a direct consequence of what just preceded it. The sleeker, more streamlined bodies are exactly why modern planes are more aerodynamic. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
